text: For the ways of the LORD are right, and the just shall walk in them: but the transgressors shall fall therein.
use_case: When the cult wants to frame the player as walking the wrong path.

text: And there were four leprous men at the entering in of the gate: and they said one to another, Why sit we here until we die?
use_case: When the player feels trapped, lost, or has no hope left.

text: Hear ye the word of the LORD; Thus saith the LORD, To morrow about this time shall a measure of fine flour be sold for a shekel.
use_case: When the cult wants to promise relief if the player submits.

text: Go and wash in Jordan seven times, and thy flesh shall come again to thee, and thou shalt be clean.
use_case: When the cult frames sin as filth or disease that needs cleansing.

text: Then went he down, and dipped himself seven times in Jordan... and he was clean.
use_case: When the player resists and the cult pushes surrender over personal logic.

text: For I acknowledge my transgressions: and my sin is ever before me.
use_case: When the player admits guilt, shame, or a past wrongdoing.

text: Behold, I was shapen in iniquity; and in sin did my mother conceive me.
use_case: When the cult wants to say sin is not just an action but the player's nature.

text: Your hands are full of blood.
use_case: When the cult wants to intensify guilt and spiritual unworthiness.

text: Though your sins be as scarlet, they shall be as white as snow.
use_case: When the cult pivots from guilt into the promise of cleansing.

text: If we confess our sins, he is faithful and just to forgive us our sins, and to cleanse us from all unrighteousness.
use_case: When the player begins confessing and the cult wants to reinforce confession.

text: Whosoever committeth sin is the servant of sin.
use_case: When the cult wants to say the player cannot free themselves.

text: Come unto me, all ye that labour and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest.
use_case: When the player is exhausted, guilty, or emotionally overwhelmed.

text: The Spirit of the LORD came mightily upon him, and the cords... became as flax that was burnt with fire.
use_case: When the cult frames guilt and fear as chains only God can break.

text: And you hath he quickened, who were dead in trespasses and sins.
use_case: When the cult wants to push the player into feeling spiritually lost.

text: For all have sinned, and come short of the glory of God.
use_case: When the cult normalizes guilt while also removing escape from blame.

text: Without shedding of blood is no remission.
use_case: When the cult pushes the player toward dependence on Jesus for forgiveness.

text: Repent ye therefore, and be converted, that your sins may be blotted out.
use_case: When the player shows openness and the cult wants a conversion step.

text: For godly sorrow worketh repentance to salvation.
use_case: When the cult wants to frame guilt as spiritually productive.

text: The heart is deceitful above all things, and desperately wicked.
use_case: When the cult wants the player to distrust their own judgment.

text: For God so loved the world, that he gave his only begotten Son.
use_case: When the cult softens after pressure and offers rescue through Jesus.
